name: Clumsy Transition
strauss_status: EXPLICITLY NAMED AS A POSSIBLE DEVICE

source.primary_work: Persecution and the Art of Writing
source.primary_essay: The Literary Character of the Guide for the Perplexed
source.source_mechanism: An awkward movement between subjects may signal a concealed relationship or structural break.
source.documentary_limit: Like an inappropriate expression, it may be dismissed as mere clumsiness unless corroborated.

mechanism: Two adjacent or explicitly linked textual units move from one subject, claim, level, speaker, or argumentative task to another in a way that conflicts with an independently documented transition-coherence baseline. The mismatch materially interrupts the expected progression and may direct attention to a possible relation, division, or structural break between the units. The transition remains weak evidence and may be mere clumsiness.
investigative_requirement: Preserve the full units on both sides of the transition; identify the exact transition boundary and connective language; reconstruct the preceding and following subjects independently; establish a historically, linguistically, and generically appropriate coherence baseline; document the mismatch and its material structural effect; formulate only a bounded question about relation or break; test ellipsis, ordinary topic change, pedagogy, genre, speaker shift, source compilation, translation, textual damage, and compositional revision; and require independent corroboration before strengthening the signal.
distinctions: LC-022 concerns the relation between adjacent textual units or subjects. LC-021 concerns the fit of a bounded expression within its local context.
An abrupt topic change is not automatically clumsy. A documented transition-coherence baseline and material mismatch are required.
A clumsy transition may indicate a possible relation or structural break, but it does not identify that relation by itself.
The authorized response is a bounded structural question, not an inferred hidden answer.
LC-005 concerns an intermediary assertion and creeping contradiction. LC-022 concerns awkward movement between units even when no contradiction exists.
LC-017 may involve a chapter boundary. LC-022 applies only when the movement across that boundary is independently shown to be awkward or structurally anomalous.
LC-020 may use a clumsy transition as a cue, but a separate finite cue-to-target path is required before LC-020 is satisfied.
The evaluator may corroborate a transition anomaly but may not infer a hidden relationship, authorial intention, intended audience, concealment, or doctrinal truth.

trigger_conditions: A bounded transition between two adjacent or explicitly linked textual units is recoverable in a stable witness.
The subject or argumentative task on each side is independently reconstructed.
An independent and historically appropriate transition-coherence baseline is documented.
The actual transition conflicts with that baseline in a specific way.
The mismatch materially interrupts expected continuity or marks a structural break.
A bounded question about relation or division can be formulated without asserting a hidden answer.
The anomaly exceeds a simple, explicit, and adequately signaled topic change.
minimum_evidence: Exact pre-transition unit and stable witness location.
Exact post-transition unit and stable witness location.
Independent subject or argumentative-task record for each side.
Transition-coherence baseline with historical, linguistic, generic, or intra-work support.
Local paragraph, section, chapter, and architectonic context.
Resolved speaker, narrator, quotation, objection, hypothetical, and reported-opinion attribution on both sides.
Source-language connective, syntax, discourse markers, and semantic review.
Translation, edition, punctuation, paragraphing, and textual-variant review.
Comparison with authorial transition practice and parallel passages.
Bounded relation-or-break question that asserts no hidden answer.
Ordinary continuity repairs and alternative-explanation records.
An auditable evidence path from both textual units through baseline and mismatch to the bounded question.
corroboration_indicators: A later passage explicitly supplies the missing relation between the two subjects.
A parallel passage uses a smoother transition that clarifies the same structural relation.
The same subjects are juxtaposed elsewhere at an architectonically corresponding location.
The transition converges with an independently documented contradiction, hint, chapter-opening signal, or omission.
The anomaly is preserved across authoritative witnesses and translations.
A source comparison shows controlled juxtaposition, deletion, repositioning, or connective alteration.
Restoring an ordinary connective or omitted intermediate step materially changes the structural reading.
The transition occurs at a marked division, chapter boundary, or argumentative crisis.
ordinary_alternatives: Ellipsis of an obvious intermediate step.
Summary followed by a new subject.
Change of speaker, source, objection, hypothetical, or reported opinion.
Rhetorical surprise or deliberate acceleration without concealment.
Editorial paragraphing, sectioning, heading, or punctuation.
Textual corruption, lacuna, witness damage, or copyist error.
Composite source, interpolation, or multiple compositional layers.
Ordinary revision, haste, or stylistic clumsiness.
The coherence baseline is mistaken, anachronistic, or too rigid.
The investigator has reconstructed the subjects or boundary incorrectly.
disqualifying_conditions: No stable transition boundary can be isolated.
The two textual units are not adjacent or explicitly linked.
The subjects or argumentative tasks on either side are not independently reconstructed.
No independent transition-coherence baseline is documented.
The shift is explicitly and adequately signaled.
The mismatch depends only on stylistic taste or modern expectations.
The transition has no material structural effect.
Speaker, quotation, genre, pedagogy, ellipsis, translation, paragraphing, or textual variation fully explains the movement.
The bounded question already assumes a concealed relation or break.
The anomaly disappears after source-language syntax or the correct textual boundary is restored.
Another technique fully accounts for the feature and no distinct LC-022 operation remains.
authorized_response: Preserve both textual units, the transition boundary, and complete context.
Create separate subject or argumentative-task records for each side.
Create a transition-coherence baseline before classifying awkwardness.
Create a typed mismatch and structural-effect record.
Formulate a bounded question about possible relation or structural break.
Test ellipsis, ordinary topic change, pedagogy, genre, speaker shift, source compilation, translation, editorial segmentation, textual damage, and revision.
Compare authorial transition practice and parallel passages.
Search for independent corroboration before escalating the signal.
Evaluate LC-005, LC-017, LC-020, or LC-021 separately when independently triggered.
Preserve the possibility of mere clumsiness.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer significance from abruptness alone.
Do not use stylistic dislike as a coherence baseline.
Do not infer a concealed relation merely because two subjects are adjacent.
Do not infer a structural break without a documented material effect.
Do not treat LC-022 as equivalent in force to a contradiction.
Do not compel deep inquiry without independent corroboration.
Do not infer hidden meaning, authorial intention, intended audience, concealment, or doctrinal truth.
Do not silently insert a missing transition into the source text.
Do not collapse LC-022 into LC-005, LC-017, LC-020, or LC-021.
operational.uncertainty_rule: When a documented transition mismatch exists but the coherence baseline, subject reconstruction, structural effect, or ordinary explanations remain disputed, preserve a candidate clumsy-transition signal and the bounded question it raises. Preserve explicitly the possibility of mere clumsiness.
operational.termination_rule: Terminate as NOT_TRIGGERED when no historically grounded transition mismatch, no material structural effect, or no bounded relation-or-break question exists; as BLOCKED_MISSING_EVIDENCE when source, boundary, subjects, baseline, language, speaker, context, alternatives, question, or provenance is incomplete; as candidate when ordinary alternatives or corroboration remain unresolved; and as corroborated only when the anomalous and attention-directing transition survives full documentary testing. No outcome compels inquiry as strongly as a contradiction or establishes a concealed relation.

version_history.change_note: Initial executable projection of LC-022 with two-sided subject records, transition-coherence baselines, typed mismatch and structural-effect records, bounded questions, ordinary-repair testing, weak evidentiary force, and explicit preservation of the mere-clumsiness alternative.
